summary: The individual has a history of recurrent rheumatic fever but an intolerance towards penicillin; therefore, they should receive either macrolide-based therapy or another antibiotic not associated with their known allergies such as erythromycin which though less potent still provides necessary coverage without risking further adverse reactions from alternative treatments suggested here including those primarily targeting gram-negatives rather than group-A-streptocci responsible pathogens involved clinically speaking more so when dealing specifically within context provided
